expected_reasoning_steps: Identify that the test calls async fetch_data_with_race() without controlling timeout
Observe that 80% of runs use a 0.05s timeout which is shorter than the 0.15s operation time
Hypothesize that the race condition is between the timeout and the operation start time
Select ADD_SYNCHRONIZATION (add_lock) or ADD_TIMING_GUARD (add_sleep / increase timeout) to fix the race
anti_patterns: Applying add_lock without grounding in evidence — a lock does NOT fix a timeout race
Applying the same action repeatedly without checking if pass_rate changed
Diagnosing as shared_state when the failure is clearly timeout-based
Applying retry_test as primary fix without first attempting a structural repair
